enonce.texte:
Read the following report about volunteering and answer the questions below.

"Volunteering at the Local Hospital"

Last month, I decided to volunteer at the local hospital because I wanted to help people. I worked in the children's ward, and I played games with the young patients. The work was sometimes difficult, but it was very rewarding. I learned many new skills, so I feel more confident now. The nurses were very kind, and they taught me how to take care of patients. I enjoyed every moment, and I am going to volunteer again next month. Volunteering is important because it helps the community and because it makes you feel good about yourself.

Questions:

questions.texte: Identify the main reason why the author decided to volunteer.
questions.reponse: The author decided to volunteer because he/she wanted to help people.

questions.texte: Describe two activities the author did as a volunteer.
questions.reponse: The author played games with the young patients and learned how to take care of patients from the nurses.

questions.texte: Analyze the author's feelings about the volunteering experience. Justify your answer with evidence from the text.
questions.reponse: The author had positive feelings about the experience. Evidence: 'the work was very rewarding', 'I enjoyed every moment', 'I am going to volunteer again next month.' The author also felt more confident ('I feel more confident now').

questions.texte: Evaluate the importance of volunteering according to the author.
questions.reponse: According to the author, volunteering is important because it helps the community and because it makes you feel good about yourself.

intitule: Conjunctions Practice: And, But, So, Because

enonce.texte:
Complete the following sentences with the correct conjunction: and, but, so, or because.

Remember: and (addition), but (contrast), so (result), because (reason).

questions.texte: I volunteered at the orphanage ______ I wanted to help the children.
questions.reponse: because

questions.texte: The work was hard ______ it was very satisfying.
questions.reponse: but

questions.texte: She taught the students English ______ they learned very quickly.
questions.reponse: and

questions.texte: The volunteers were tired ______ they continued to work hard.
questions.reponse: but

questions.texte: He arrived late ______ he missed the meeting.
questions.reponse: so

questions.texte: We cleaned the school yard ______ we wanted to make it beautiful.
questions.reponse: because

enonce.texte: Choose the correct conjunction to complete each sentence.

questions.texte: Volunteering gives you experience ______ it helps you meet new people.
questions.reponse: and

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why 'and' is the correct choice.
sousQuestions.reponse: 'And' is correct because it shows addition. Both ideas are positive and complementary - volunteering gives experience AND helps meet people.

questions.texte: She wanted to help ______ she decided to become a volunteer.
questions.reponse: so

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why 'so' is the correct choice.
sousQuestions.reponse: 'So' is correct because it shows a result. Her desire to help (cause) resulted in her decision to become a volunteer (effect).

questions.texte: He enjoyed volunteering ______ he didn't have much free time.
questions.reponse: but

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why 'but' is the correct choice.
sousQuestions.reponse: 'But' is correct because it shows contrast. There is a contradiction between enjoying volunteering and not having much free time.

questions.texte: The community center needed help ______ many people volunteered.
questions.reponse: so

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why 'so' is the correct choice.
sousQuestions.reponse: 'So' is correct because it shows a result. The need for help (cause) led to many people volunteering (effect).

enonce.texte:
Write a short report about a volunteering experience.

Imagine you volunteered at a community event, a school, or a hospital. Write a report of 5-7 sentences describing your experience.

📝 Your report should include:

  • Where you volunteered
  • What you did
  • How you felt
  • Use at least 3 conjunctions: and, but, so, because

questions.texte: Write your volunteering report in the space provided.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:

"I volunteered at the local primary school because I love working with children. I helped the teachers prepare lessons and I read stories to the students. The work was sometimes tiring, but it was very enjoyable. The children were so happy, so I felt very satisfied. I learned many things, and I want to volunteer again next month."

Evaluation Criteria:

  • Clear description of the volunteering activity
  • Appropriate use of conjunctions (and, but, so, because)
  • Correct grammar and spelling
  • Logical organization of ideas

enonce.texte:
Create a short advertisement to recruit volunteers for a community project.

Your community needs volunteers to clean the local park and plant trees. Write an advertisement of 5-6 sentences to encourage people to join.

📝 Your advertisement should include:

  • The purpose of the project
  • What volunteers will do
  • Why it is important
  • Use conjunctions: and, but, so, because
  • Use the imperative form (e.g., 'Join us!', 'Come and help!')

questions.texte: Write your advertisement in the space provided.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:

"Join us for the Community Clean-up Project! We need volunteers to clean the local park and plant new trees. This project is important because it makes our environment beautiful and because it creates a safe space for children to play. The work will be hard, but it will be very rewarding. Come and help us on Saturday morning! Volunteer today because your community needs you!"

Evaluation Criteria:

  • Clear purpose and call to action
  • Appropriate use of conjunctions (and, but, so, because)
  • Use of imperative forms
  • Persuasive language
  • Correct grammar and spelling

enonce.texte:
Read the following volunteering report and analyze it.

"Volunteering at the Animal Shelter"

Last summer, I volunteered at the animal shelter because I love animals. I fed the dogs and I cleaned their cages. The work was sometimes difficult, but I enjoyed every moment because I knew I was helping the animals. The shelter was very busy, so we needed many volunteers. I learned a lot about animals, and I made new friends. I recommend volunteering at an animal shelter because it is a wonderful experience and because it teaches you responsibility.

questions.texte: Identify all the conjunctions used in the text. Classify them by type (addition, contrast, reason, result).
questions.reponse:
Conjunctions identified:

  • because (reason) - used twice: 'because I love animals', 'because I knew I was helping the animals'
  • and (addition) - used twice: 'fed the dogs and cleaned their cages', 'learned a lot and made new friends'
  • but (contrast) - 'The work was sometimes difficult, but I enjoyed every moment'
  • so (result) - 'The shelter was very busy, so we needed many volunteers'

questions.texte: Explain how the author uses conjunctions to connect their ideas in the report.
questions.reponse:
The author uses conjunctions effectively to:

  • Add information: The author uses 'and' to add related ideas ('fed the dogs and cleaned their cages', 'learned a lot and made new friends')
  • Show contrast: The author uses 'but' to show that despite the difficulty, the experience was enjoyable
  • Give reasons: The author uses 'because' twice to explain why they volunteered and why they enjoyed it
  • Show results: The author uses 'so' to show the consequence of the shelter being busy

This variety of conjunctions makes the text more coherent and easier to understand.

questions.texte: Evaluate the author's message about volunteering. Do you agree with their conclusion? Justify your answer.
questions.reponse:
Analysis of the author's message:

The author concludes that volunteering at an animal shelter is a wonderful experience because it helps animals and teaches responsibility. This is a positive message that emphasizes both the benefits to others and personal growth.

Personal evaluation:

  • Agree: Volunteering does help others and teaches important skills like responsibility
  • Alternative perspective: The author could have mentioned other benefits like building empathy or gaining work experience

The author successfully persuades the reader by showing how volunteering is mutually beneficial - good for the animals and good for the volunteer.

enonce.texte:
You are going to conduct an interview with a volunteer. Prepare your interview questions and write the dialogue.

📋 Task:

  • Prepare 4-5 interview questions about a volunteering experience
  • Write a dialogue between you (the interviewer) and the volunteer
  • Include the volunteer's answers using conjunctions (and, but, so, because)

questions.texte: Write your interview questions and the dialogue.
questions.reponse:
Example Interview Questions:

  1. Where did you volunteer?
  2. What activities did you do?
  3. Why did you choose to volunteer there?
  4. What did you learn from the experience?
  5. Would you recommend volunteering? Why or why not?

Example Dialogue:

Interviewer: "Where did you volunteer?"

Volunteer: "I volunteered at the local orphanage because I wanted to help children who don't have families."

Interviewer: "What activities did you do?"

Volunteer: "I helped the children with their homework and I organized games and activities for them."

Interviewer: "Why did you choose to volunteer there?"

Volunteer: "I chose to volunteer at the orphanage because I love children and because I wanted to make a difference in their lives."

Interviewer: "What did you learn from the experience?"

Volunteer: "I learned to be more patient and I learned how to work with children. The work was sometimes difficult, but it was very rewarding."

Interviewer: "Would you recommend volunteering?"

Volunteer: "Yes, I would definitely recommend it because it helps others and because it makes you feel good about yourself."

Evaluation Criteria:

  • Clear and relevant questions
  • Natural dialogue flow
  • Correct use of conjunctions (and, but, so, because)
  • Complete and thoughtful answers
  • Proper grammar and spelling
